attacking_style: Excels in ultra-long breakaways (185km documented), combining diesel engine endurance with finishing speed to outlast companions. Capable of strong individual finishes without team support in Classics.
team_tactics: Historically under-utilized as domestique despite top-tier capacity; positioned for crosswind splits and sprint leadouts rather than protected status. Soudal shift in 2023 suggests evolving prioritization.
terrain_preference: Cobbled Classics terrain (RVV second place) and long, attrition-based stages where superior diesel power and tactical patience outlast punchier rivals. Crosswind situations play to positioning instincts.
vulnerability: Sprint team misjudgments can negate breakaway efforts if caught on the line. Past team role confusion limited results despite individual strength, suggesting need for clear protected status.
key_patterns: Consistent underestimation by rivals: sprint teams misjudge breakaway threats, teams undervalue his capacity (2 instances)
Strong individual results without team support: second at RVV, Tour stage win both show autonomy (2 races)
Elite positioning instincts: immediate second-wheel placement in crosswinds, breakaway selection timing (2 documented)
Late-career trajectory: first Tour stage win in fifth attempt at age 28+ suggests delayed recognition (2023 breakthrough)
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Tour stage win from long breakaway represents breakthrough as protected rider after years as underutilized domestique, suggesting peaked maturity and tactical confidence
